clues: This was discovered accidentally by a second year medical student, Jay McLean, at Johns Hopkins University in 1916 who was working with dog liver. The name is also derived from this discovery
The global supply chain is surprisingly fragile because it is extracted almost entirely from the intestinal mucosa of pigs. It takes roughly 2,000 pigs to yield a single kilogram of this medication
This medication acts by binding to the plasma protein antithrombin

In the 16th to 17th century, the first attempts at this involved the use of tobacco enemas to help drowning victims. This technique was heavily promoted and in Venice, was legally required with bystanders and officials facing fines if they failed to perform the enema when appropriate

A young woman drowned in the Seine and was never identified. She was nicknamed 'the Mona Lisa of the Seine' for her serene expression and a Norwegian toy manufacturer used her face for a doll used to train people in this procedure

Michael Jackson took a class in this which inspired the famous lyric 'Annie, are you okay' from his song 'Smooth Criminal' since you're taught to check for responsiveness. You can also sing the song in your head while doing this procedure since it has a tempo between 100-120 bpm, although you might prefer 'Staying Alive' or 'Another One Hits the Dust'

Before this was discovered, patients were treated with starvation diets designed to prolong life by a few months. This medication was discovered and tested in 1922 and today, an estimated 8.3 million people in the United States require this medication

Interestingly, the predatory cone snail uses similar peptides to paralyze nearby fish. These fish become lethargic and display erratic, uncoordinated movements allowing the snail to easily engulf and consume them. These symptoms are similar to what a human might display if given a large dose of this medication
While the original patent for this drug was only $1, this life-saving medication was on the news for being unaffordable until costs were capped at $35 per month for Medicare beneficiaries in 2022

This medication is frequently used off label for anxiety, such as during public speaking. It is strictly prohibited in competition by the World Anti-Doping Agency in sports such as archery, shooting, and billiards
One of the top ten most prescribed medications in the United States, this drug is known for causing vivid dreams and erectile dysfunction
This beta-1 selective medication has different clinical uses depending on whether it is formulated as an immediate- or extended-release preparation.

This organism was first observed in 1909 by Brazilian physician Carlos Chagas, who mistakenly identified the cysts as part of the life cycle of Trypanosoma cruzi, the parasite responsible for Chagas disease
Following WWII, outbreaks of a fatal pneumonia caused by this erupted across European orphanages affecting premature and malnourished infants

On June 5, 1981, the CDC published about five cases of pneumonia caused by this fungus in young, previously healthy gay men in LA. this was the first documented public record of what would later be known as AIDS
